classification.ap_topic_title: Defining Limits and Using Limit Notation
classification.framework: AP Calculus Course and Exam Description, current Unit 1 topic framework

questions.prompt_html:
Solve the problem.

What conditions, when present, are sufficient to conclude that a function f(x) has a limit as x approaches some value of a?
questions.prompt_text: Solve the problem. What conditions, when present, are sufficient to conclude that a function f(x) has a limit as x approaches some value of a?

choices.label: A
choices.html: The limit of  as  from the left exists, the limit of  as  from the right exists, and at least one of these limits is the same as f(a).
choices.text: The limit of f(x) as x→a from the left exists, the limit of f(x) as x→a from the right exists, and at least one of these limits is the same as f(a).

choices.label: B
choices.html: f(a) exists, the limit of  as  from the left exists, and the limit of  as  from the right exists.
choices.text: f(a) exists, the limit of f(x) as x→a from the left exists, and the limit of f(x) as x→a from the right exists.

choices.label: C
choices.html: The limit of  as  from the left exists, the limit of  as  from the right exists, and these two limits are the same.
choices.text: The limit of f(x) as x→a from the left exists, the limit of f(x) as x→a from the right exists, and these two limits are the same.

choices.label: D
choices.html: Either the limit of  as  from the left exists or the limit of  as  from the right exists
choices.text: Either the limit of f(x) as x→a from the left exists or the limit of f(x) as x→a from the right exists

questions.solution_html:
First observe that x4 - k4 = (x2 - k2 )(x2 + k2 ) = (x - k)(x + k)(x2 + k2 )
Hence, =  = (x + k)(x2 + k2 ) = (2k)(2k2) = 4k3
Now 4k3 = -32 ⇒ k3 = -8, therefore k =  = -2.
